title: sphaeralcea coccinea subsp. elata
intro: Sphaeralcea coccinea subsp. elata, also known as the tall orange globe mallow, is a perennial plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, derived from its roots and flowers. The health benefits of Sphaeralcea coccinea subsp. elata include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
